description: Extend the shared Workflow V2 trigger schema to support one-time schedule and recurring schedule trigger variants while preserving no-trigger as an absent trigger
Update workflow create and update server-action schemas to accept the new time-trigger variants
Enforce EE-only availability for time-triggered workflows in server-side create, update, and publish paths
Register a fixed synthetic payload schema contract for time-triggered workflow runs
Require pinned payload schema mode for time-triggered workflows and block inferred mode
Validate one-time schedule trigger definitions including required future timestamp semantics
Validate recurring schedule trigger definitions including timezone and 5-field cron syntax with no seconds support
Replace trigger-type heuristics in workflow list and query paths with real persisted trigger type values
Add a trigger-type selector to Workflow Designer with No trigger, Event, One-time schedule, and Recurring schedule options
Add Workflow Designer configuration UI for one-time schedule triggers
Add Workflow Designer configuration UI for recurring schedule triggers with timezone selection and 5-field cron guidance
Hide event catalog and trigger-mapping controls when a workflow uses a time trigger
Show the fixed time-trigger payload contract in Workflow Designer when a time trigger is selected
Add an EE workflow schedule state table for time-trigger registration and lifecycle tracking
Persist job-runner identifiers and schedule lifecycle state for registered workflow schedules
Extract a shared workflow launcher service that centralizes run-start behavior across trigger types
Refactor the existing event-trigger ingress path to use the shared launcher service
Add an EE job handler that starts one-time scheduled workflow runs through the shared launcher
Add an EE job handler that starts recurring scheduled workflow runs through the shared launcher
Register one-time scheduled jobs when a published workflow uses a one-time time trigger
Register recurring schedules when a published workflow uses a recurring time trigger
Cancel or disable registered time-trigger jobs when workflows are paused, deleted, or changed away from time triggers
Atomically reschedule published time-trigger registrations when trigger configuration changes
Ensure one-time schedules fire at most once and transition to a completed state after firing
Persist time-trigger run provenance so workflow run APIs and UI can distinguish one-time and recurring clock starts
Update workflow runs and workflow summary UI to display time-trigger labels and schedule state for time-triggered workflows
Add duplicate-fire protection so retries or repeated delivery of the same schedule fire cannot create duplicate workflow runs
